title: Blake Lively's Sexual Harassment Claims Against Justin Baldoni Dismissed, but Case Remains Strong: WRAL Report
body:
A federal judge in New York has dealt a blow to Blake Lively's high-profile lawsuit against producer Justin Baldoni, dismissing claims of sexual harassment related to their collaboration on the movie "It Ends With Us." However, the judge left intact three other claims, including allegations of retaliation, leaving the door open for further action in the case.

The decision by Judge Lewis J. Liman in Manhattan's federal court marks a significant setback for Lively, who had accused Baldoni of creating a hostile work environment on set and subjecting her to unwanted advances. The actress had allegedly made these claims as part of a broader lawsuit filed against the production company behind the film, which is based on the bestselling novel by Colleen Hoover. However, the judge's ruling dismissed these allegations of sexual harassment, citing a lack of evidence to support them.

Despite the setback, Lively's lawyers have expressed optimism that the remaining claims will prevail in court. The three intact claims include allegations of retaliation, as well as complaints about the production company's handling of Lively's requests for accommodations and working conditions. These claims are seen as potentially more damaging to the production company's reputation and could have serious implications for the future of the film's marketing and distribution.

The controversy surrounding "It Ends With Us" has been ongoing for months, with Lively's allegations of a toxic work environment and Baldoni's denials sparking a heated debate in the entertainment industry. The movie, which is set to be released later this year, has already generated significant buzz due to its high-profile cast and the sensitive subject matter. However, the ongoing legal battle has cast a shadow over the film's potential success and has raised questions about the production company's commitment to creating a safe and respectful work environment.

As the case continues to unfold, it remains to be seen how the production company will respond to the remaining claims. The company has thus far maintained a silence on the matter, with representatives declining to comment on the judge's ruling. Lively's lawyers, on the other hand, have vowed to continue fighting for their client's rights and to bring the truth about the alleged mistreatment on set to light.

As the entertainment industry continues to grapple with issues of sexual harassment and workplace misconduct, the outcome of this case will be closely watched by industry insiders and fans alike. The decision to dismiss the claims of sexual harassment but leave the retaliation claims intact serves as a reminder that the pursuit of justice and accountability is often a complex and nuanced process. As the case moves forward, it will be essential to keep a close eye on the developments and to continue the conversation about the importance of creating safe and respectful workplaces in the entertainment industry.
